item.title: Biocatalytic, Enantioselective Oxa-Pictet-Spengler Reactions for the Asymmetric Synthesis of Tetrahydropyranoindoles
item.description: We report that ZmoSHC1 exhibits new-to-nature activity for oxa-Pictet-Spengler reactions using tryptophol vinyl ethers as substrates. Directed evolution is used to engineer a single mutant, ZmoSHC1 Y702L, which demonstrates high activity (up to 3370 TTN) ...

item.title: A Density Matrix Theory for Photothermal Two-Dimensional Infrared Spectroscopy
item.description: Two-dimensional infrared (2D-IR) spectroscopy can resolve vibrational anharmonicity, mode coupling, and energy transfer. The conventional 2D-IR detects nonlinear polarization that generates propagating photons after sample’s light matter interaction with ...

item.title: Bismuth-Catalyzed Carbonylation of C-N Bonds in Tertiary Aniline Derivatives
item.description: A novel catalytic system is presented that uses readily producible or commercially available bismuth iodides as catalysts to carbonylate tertiary aniline derivatives into their corresponding tertiary amides. This challenging reaction demonstrates the ...

item.title: Cristobalite-Type Compounds for Rechargeable Potassium-Ion Batteries
item.description: Potassium-ion batteries have emerged as high-voltage and economically advantageous electrochemical energy storage systems with promise to viably complement and/or augment lithium-ion technology owing to the natural abundance, projected low cost and ...

item.title: 25 Years of Cardiac Ion Channel QSAR: From hERG Dominance to Multi-Channel Modelling
item.description: In 2013 the Comprehensive in vitro Proarrhythmia Assay (CiPA) initiative proposed multi-channel assessment for proarrhythmic risk evaluation, yet ligand-based predictive modelling has remained largely single-channel. To quantify and explain this mismatch, ...

item.description: Understanding how aggregation state and metal identity affect excited-state dynamics of phthalocyanines (Pcs) is essential for rationalising their performance as photosensitisers in phototherapeutic applications. Here, we investigate the steady-state and ...

item.title: Armstrong's Range Exponent Electron Configuration Model (AREECM)
item.description: Traditional electron-configuration notation (e.g., 1s^2 2s^2 2p^6) represents multi-electron systems using integer orbital occupations that reflect dominant configurations but do not explicitly encode the multi-configurational nature of correlated ...

item.title: KinSPEC: A Kinase Sequence-Based Prior-Guided Framework for Predicting Compound-Protein Interactions
item.description: In silico virtual screening has become an important strategy in early-stage drug discovery for identifying compound-protein interactions (CPIs). Structure-based and ligand-based approaches are widely used but rely heavily on the availability of high-...

item.title: Conformation-Guided Disulfide Pairing Enables Efficient Folding of Disulfide-Rich Peptides
item.description: Disulfide bond formation is critical for the structural integrity and biological functions of peptides and proteins. For disulfide-rich peptides (DRPs), particularly those containing ≥ 3 disulfide bonds, this process remains challenging due to misfolding ...

item.title: Cobaloxime-Based Metalloradical Catalysis for C(sp3)–Si bond formation via Carbene Insertion into Hydrosilanes
item.description: Organosilicon compounds serve as valuable scaffolds with extensive applications across organic synthesis, materials chemistry, and pharmaceuticals. We demonstrate that cobaloximes effectively catalyze carbene radical insertion into Si–H bonds, utilizing α-...

item.title: Unveiling Nb2O5 as a Catalytically Functional Oxide for Thermocatalytic CO2 Hydrogenation
item.description: The thermocatalytic hydrogenation of CO2 typically relies on reducible oxides such as CeO2, TiO2, ZrO2 and In2O3 to achieve CO2 adsorption and activation, especially the cleavage of C=O bonds. Here, we report that niobium pentoxide (Nb2O5), when ...

item.title: Integrating Molecular Docking into Environmental Bioremediation Research: A Study of Fungal Enzyme–Pesticide Interactions
item.description: The extensive use of pesticides in agriculture has resulted in widespread environmental contamination, threatening ecosystems and human health. Bioremediation using fungal oxidative enzymes represents an eco-friendly approach for pesticide detoxification. ...

item.title: Competitive Coordination Reveals Organofluoride-Stabilized SEI in All-Solid-State Lithium Metal Batteries with Polymer Electrolyte

item.title: Interfacial structure of conjugated polymer photocatalysts with water and scavengers for sacrificial hydrogen evolution
item.description: Conjugated polymers have emerged as competitive photocatalysts for the sustainable production of hydrogen from water. Their synthetic versatility allows for systematic tuning of their opto-electronic properties and polarity to improve visible-light-driven ...

item.title: Switching Reactivity with Light: C–P Bonding Controls Singlet Oxygen Generation and Reactivity in DHP–CPD Photoswitches
item.description: The dimethyldihydropyrene photochromic system is a 14π-electronic framework that switches to a lessaromatic non-planar system with visible light. In this work we unravel a neutral phosphorus heteroatom-appended dimethyldihydropyrene (DHP) photoswitchable ...

item.description: The integration of CO2 capture with residual biomass valorization offers a promising route to sustainable, multifunctional materials for cosmetic and surface protection applications. In this work, biobased nanocomposites were developed by combining two ...

item.description: Ragone plots provide a compact representation of energy storage performance by relating accessible energy to power. Despite this, battery degradation is often described using simplified metrics such as “energy fade” and “power fade”, which obscure changes ...

item.description: Understanding charge carrier dynamics in granular photocatalysts is critical for rational materials design, yet conventional spectroscopies lack the spatial directionality needed to distinguish individual electron and hole contributions. Here, time-...

item.title: Observation of ”negative” persistent photoconductivity effect at T > 200 oC in N-polar GaN/AlGaN/GaN heterostructures
item.description: It is rarely reported that semiconductor materials can exhibit the ”negative” persistent photoconductivity (PC) effect. In this work the ”negative” persistent PC effect was observed in the Nitrogen (N)-polar oriented GaN/AlGaN/GaN heterostructure under ...

item.title: Kinetics Beyond Pose: A Structure-independent Framework for koff Prediction Across Diverse Protein Families
item.description: Predicting the ligand–receptor dissociation rate constant (koff) remains to this day a key challenge in drug discovery, despite its well-established relevance to residence time and in vivo drug efficacy, since it depends on the dynamic interaction between ...

item.title: TD-DCC: An Interactive and Efficient Graphical User Interface-Based Time-Dependent Cross-Correlation Analysis from Molecular Dynamics Trajectories
item.description: Dynamic cross-correlation matrix (DCCM) analysis is a widely used method for inferring correlated motions in biological macromolecules from molecular dynamics (MD) trajectories. Conventional implementations often result in a single time-averaged matrix ...

item.title: Chlorinated Byproducts in Diisocyanates Synthesis from 35Cl NQR
item.description: Chlorinated impurities in industrial diisocyanates production significantly impact product quality and stability, yet their speciation remains an analytical challenge. This study demonstrates that 35Cl NQR spectroscopy, supported by DFT calculations, ...

item.title: Quantifying Fragment Ambiguity in LC-HRMS/MS Spectral Databases
item.description: LC–HRMS/MS-based non-target analysis relies heavily on MS2 spectral matching for compound annotation. However, not all fragments contribute equally to identification confidence, and quantitative measures of fragment ambiguity remain lacking. In this study,...

item.title: Obtaining Dynamics of Supported Metal Nanoparticles using Cost-effective Training of Machine Learning Force Fields
item.description: Metal–support interactions (MSI) play a critical role in heterogeneous catalysis by influencing catalyst stability and reactivity. A long-term objective is to model catalytic systems in simulated experiments, to obtain atomistic insights into MSI under ...

item.title: In situ salt-loaded cathode by mechanochemical modification for pre-metallation of next-generation metal-ion capacitors
item.description: Metal-ion capacitors (MICs) as hybrid devices stand out for their high energy and power output. Their Achilles’ heel, however, is the mandatory pre-metallation, which is essential for the device to operate at its best. Depending on the chosen strategy, it ...

item.title: Unexpected Photochemical Synthesis of Unsubstituted AzaBenzannulated Perylenediimides and Bisazacoronene
item.description: Unsubstituted nitrogen-doped coronene diimides (CDIs), named bisazacoronene diimides (uBACDs), were unexpectedly obtained by exposing perylene diimides (PDIs) linked to a pyrrole or furan derivative via an imine bond in their bay region. This cleavage ...

item.title: Interfacial Self-Assembly of Graphene Networks at 1 vol% Enables Piezoresistive and Electrothermal Silicone Foams
item.description: Segregated network composites achieve electrical percolation at minimal filler loadings by localizing conductivity to architectural boundaries. However, achieving this architecture typically requires tedious multi-step processing, often incompatible with ...

item.title: A Synergistic Multimodal DEL-Driven Strategy with In Silico Guidance for SARS-CoV-2 PLpro Inhibitor Discovery
item.description: The SARS-CoV-2 papain-like protease (PLpro) is a pivotal antiviral target due to its essential roles in viral replication and host immune evasion. Here, we report a synergistic, multimodal strategy driven by DNA-encoded libraries (DEL) and integrated with ...

item.title: An efficient selective synthesis of E isomers of di-SF4-vinyl fluorides from pyridine-SF4-acetylene in presence of Triethyl borane.
item.description: The synthesis of vinyl fluorides plays a crucial role in various scientific disciplines, including pharmaceutical and materials sciences. In this study, first time we present a direct and stereoselective method for the synthesis E isomers of Di-SF4-vinyl ...

item.title: Accelerated characterization of active natural extracts with single-step microfractionation coupled to mass spectrometry and bioassays
item.description: Microfractionation is a prominent alternative for the discovery and characterization of bioactive natural products in complex extracts (e.g., plant, fungal, or microbial); however, the method faces limitations as it requires arduous scale-up for complete ...

item.title: Production of Metallophores Rhodopetrobactin B and Methylolanthanin in Methylobacterium extorquens strains PA1 and AM1
item.description: The closely related M. extorquens strains PA1 and AM1 carry near-identical metallophore synthesis gene clusters, yet AM1 produces the 4-hydroxy benzoate (4HB)-capped chelator methylolanthanin (MLL), while PA1 produces mainly 3,4-dihydroxy benzoate (3,4-...

item.title: Acyl-Chain Unsaturation in the Cremimycin Macrolactam Yields Cremimycin B with

item.description: Laboratory-source angle-resolved hard X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (AR-HAXPES) is photon-starved atthespatialresolution required formapping,wheretheframe averaging needed to recover a clean depth profile at every position drives total acquisition time ...

item.title: Hierarchically Porous Nanoparticle-based Aerogels with Engineered Backbone Porosity for Enhanced Stability and Functionality
item.description: Precise control over the structural architecture of aerogels remains a central challenge, particularly for integrating well-defined nanoscale porosity within the aerogel backbone. Here, a nanoparticleassembly strategy is presented for fabricating aerogels ...

item.title: Predicting Amide-I Infrared Spectra Directly from Coarse-Grained Protein Simulations
item.description: Theoretical vibrational spectroscopy methods are often necessary to interpret condensed-phase spectra. For some methods, such as those that use vibrational spectroscopic maps, the requisite atomistic molecular dynamics simulations can become prohibitively ...

item.title: The need for direction of metabolomics research in antihyperglycemic natural product: An insight from scoping review
item.description: The global prevalence of diabetes mellitus continues to rise, underscoring the urgent need for more effective therapies. While natural products (NPs) serve as promising multi-compound multi-target antihyperglycemic candidates, the current trajectory of ...

item.title: Ultra-Efficient Ortho-Para Hydrogen Conversion by Ni-MOF-74
How Molecular Weight Shapes the Relative Importance of Chemical and Structural Descriptors for Aqueous Solubility
item.description: Learning aqueous solubility remains a fundamental challenge in drug discovery, as it directly influences oral bioavailability and the selection of viable drug candidates. While data-driven approaches have achieved considerable success, conventional ...

item.title: N-Heterocyclic Carbene-Supported Metaphosphoric Acids as Tunable Anionic Oxygen-Donor Ligands
item.description: Anionic oxygen-donor ligands play a central role in coordination chemistry, yet the development of new, modular ligand families remains limited. Herein, we report a series of N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC)-supported metaphosphoric acids (NHC→PO3H) spanning ...

item.title: End-to-end discovery of MOFs for ambient CH₄ adsorption
item.description: We report an end-to-end computational–experimental workflow for the discovery of metal–organic frameworks (MOFs), demonstrated by the computational design and synthesis of two novel Zn-based frameworks, UCHI-1 and UCHI-2, exhibiting enhanced methane ...

item.title: Chemically Auditable Failure Atlases for Trustworthy MOF Adsorption Machine Learning
item.description: Machine-learning models are now widely used to rank metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) for gas adsorption, but an attractive global error does not reveal whether a specific pore regime, topology family or chemistry class can be trusted during screening. Here ...

item.title: Beyond Trial and Error: Accelerated Bayesian Design of Dental Adhesives
item.description: The discovery and optimization of formulation-based materials are increasingly constrained by the high experimental cost and environmental burden associated with trial-and-error exploration of large, high-dimensional design spaces. Here, we present a ...

item.title: Dispersionless range-separated hybrid meta-GGA functional
item.description: We present a new dispersionless, range-separated hybrid meta-generalized gradient approximation (meta-GGA) density-functional theory (DFT) approach, dlωB97M, augmented with the D20 as-mod dispersion function (a modification of D20 as [J. Phys. Chem. A, ...

item.title: In Operando Electrochemical Reduction of Indium Tin Oxide Contacts in Silicon Oxide Memristive Devices
item.description: We report a study of current transient behaviour in silicon oxide memristive devices with indium tin oxide (ITO) top contacts. Constant-current electrical stress reveals that ITO-contacted devices exhibit chaotic conductance fluctuations and a single-...

item.title: Engineering Paediatric Formulations: A Systematic Review of Process Systems Methods, Sectoral Gaps, and the PPES Framework
item.description: Background: Children constitute a distinct physiological population, and conventional adult-focused manufacturing approaches are poorly suited to meeting their formulation needs. Process Systems Engineering (PSE) — which encompasses quality-by-design (QbD)...

item.title: The Use of PFAS in Drug Product Presentations including Formulations, Primary and Secondary Packaging, and Delivery Devices: The Survey Conducted by the IQ Consortium Drug Product PFAS Working Group to Review the Potential Impact of PFAS Restrictions on Patient Access to Medicines
item.description: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are widely present across the pharmaceutical drug product supply chain, yet their role and prevalence remain incompletely characterized. An industry survey conducted by the IQ Drug Product PFAS working group (...

item.title: Development of a Screening Workflow for the Halogenation of Nitrogen Heterocycles with Vanadium-Dependent Haloperoxidases
item.description: The halogenation of nitrogen heterocycles is a privileged transformation for accessing important synthetic intermediates and biologically active targets in medicinal chemistry. Despite the importance of N-heterocycle halogenation, current methods rely on ...

item.title: Ultra coarse-grained modeling of chiral molecular fluids
item.description: Molecular chirality is ubiquitous in nature and a signature of life. Understanding molecular chiral symmetry breaking has critical applications in pharmaceuticals, catalysis, functional soft materials, and biomolecular assemblies. Molecular dynamics ...

item.title: Near-micron single-crystalline Mn-rich cathodes with exceptional cycling stability
item.description: Achieving high-energy-density and long-term cycling stability in Mn-redox-based cathodes is a critical step toward advancing Ni- and Co-free Li-ion chemistries to diversify supply chains and lower battery costs. Here, we demonstrate an unusually high ...

item.title: Do electrostatic forces correlate with dispersion forces in charged molecular systems?
item.description: Electrostatic and dispersion forces in molecular systems have traditionally been considered independent, where each force arises from distinctly different mechanisms. However, ionic liquid systems, in which electrostatic interactions are particularly ...

item.title: Molybdenum pincer complex based reduction of dinitrogen: Mechanistic bifurcation at the stage of Mo(I)-N2 intermediates
item.description: The synthesis and characterization of a new PPP ligand, bis(di-cyclopentylphosphinoethyl)phenylphosphine (PPP-Cyp), and its derived molybdenum complexes [MoBr3(PPP-Cyp)], [Mo≡NCl2(PPP-Cyp)], [Mo≡NBr2(PPP-Cyp)] and [Mo(η6-toluene)(PPP-Cyp)] are presented. ...

item.title: Knowledge-Graph-Guided Design of Compositionally Flexible Alloys for Sustainable Metal Recycling
item.description: Developing engineering alloys that retain target properties despite compositional variability in mixed metallic scrap remains a major challenge in sustainable alloy design. Machine learning can accelerate alloy development, but limited datasets, ...

item.title: Functional Upcycling of Waste by Thermo-Electromagnetic Autocatalysis
item.description: Industrial wastes, particularly electronic wastes, pose major environmental and societal challenges owing to their rapidly increasing annual generation approaching hundreds of millions of tonnes1, compositional complexity, toxicity and low recycling rates...

item.title: KANA AI: A Chemical-Informed Neural Network with Hard Thermodynamic Constraints for Multicomponent Activity Coefficient Prediction
item.description: Accurate activity coefficient prediction (ln γ) for multicomponent mixtures is essential for compu-tational deep eutectic solvent (DES) discovery, yet existing methods are either thermodynamically inconsistent (black-box machine learning) or ...

item.title: Interfacial structure of conjugated polymer photocatalysts with water and scavengers for sacrificial hydrogen evolution
item.description: Conjugated polymers have emerged as competitive photocatalysts for the sustainable production of hydrogen from water. Their synthetic versatility allows for systematic tuning of their opto-electronic properties and polarity to improve visible-light-driven ...

item.title: Hyper-Porous Membranes for Nano-Confinement-Driven Chromatographic Uranium Trapping
item.description: Most conventional membrane and ion-exchange technologies fail to efficiently remediate ultradilute radioactive actinides in complex aquatic systems because of severe charge shielding by background electrolytes and diffusion-limited kinetics. Here, we ...

item.title: Interfacial interpenetration underlies cooperative deformation and toughness in polymer nanoparticle assemblies
item.description: Polymer materials containing numerous interfaces are often assumed to exhibit limited mechanical robustness. Here, we demonstrate that polymer nanoparticle assemblies can achieve exceptionally high toughness, with fracture strains above 1400%, tensile ...

item.title: Revealing Li growth modes using X-ray nano-tomography
item.description: To pave way for application of Li-metal electrodes in high energy density batteries the evolution of the 3D structures formed during charging needs accurate characterization inside working cells. The growth of porous, weakly connected structures is ...

item.title: AGENTi: APCI-ESI Analyte Ionization Fidelity Pre-Screening Workflow using Multimodel Inference
item.description: To realize separation and detection by mass spectrometry a neutral analyte is converted to an ion by an ionization source, which can occur either in solution or in the gas phase. The two most common methods to achieve solution or gas phase ionization are ...

item.title: Experimental and Computational Studies of Intraresidue Hydrogen Bonding Mimicry of Chloroalkene Dipeptide Isosteres
item.description: Peptide bond isosteres are effective tools for investigating backbone-mediated interactions in peptides and proteins. (Z)-chloroalkene dipeptide isosteres (CADIs) have emerged as promising amide bond surrogates; however, their H-bonding properties are ...

item.title: Modular Synthesis of Branched Aliphatic Aldehydes via Unified Iron-Photocatalyzed Decarboxylation and C-H Activation
item.description: We report a concise and practical method for the synthesis of branched aliphatic aldehydes via Iron-Ligand to Metal Charge Transfer (Fe-LMCT) catalysis. Fe-LMCT catalysis has emerged as a sustainable and highly versatile platform in organic synthesis by ...

item.title: Thermo-Electrohydrodynamics of Confined Droplets
item.description: A theoretical and analytical investigation of the coupled electrohydrodynamic and thermocapillary behaviour of a confined leaky dielectric droplet suspended in an immiscible liquid medium is presented within the creeping-flow regime using the small-...

item.title: Donor-Acceptor Conjugated Copolymers as pH-Responsive Chromatic Indicators: A TDDFT and Colorimetric Screening Study
item.description: Color change upon change of pH is a key indicator for smart packaging materials that signal food spoilage. Donor-acceptor (D–A) conjugated copolymers are attractive candidates for such indicators, as their charge-transfer transitions are highly sensitive ...

item.title: β-Stereoselective Glycosylation without Acyl Participation: Evaluation of β-(1→2)-Linked Disaccharide Donors and Their Application to Glycoarray Design for Antibody Characterization
item.description: β-(1→2)-Glucans constitute a relatively rare and substantially underexplored class of polysaccharides. Of particular interest is the macrocyclic β-(1→2)-glucan from Brucella (CβG), which regarded as a promising diagnostic marker of brucellosis. However, ...

item.title: Prioritizing early opportunities to enhance carbon efficiency at biorefineries
item.description: Biorefineries are a critical component of society’s transition from fossil-derived to bio-derived fuel and chemical production, but their life cycle carbon efficiency is often undermined by the underutilization of biomass feedstocks during processing. In ...

item.title: Reaction Dynamics of OH + SiH4 → H2O + SiH3: A Quasi-Classical Trajectory Study on the PES-2022 Surface
item.description: A quasi-classical trajectory (QCT) study is presented of the OH + SiH4→ H2O + SiH3hydrogen-abstraction reaction on the full-dimensional analytical potential energy surface PES-2022. A total of 1.8 million trajectories were run at seven fixed collision ...

item.title: Time-resolved Green Chemistry Metrics for Low Energy Depolymerization of Polyethylene Terephthalate
item.description: For the circular utilization of plastics such as polyethylene terephthalate (PET), chemical recycling has emerged as a promising approach for processing diverse waste streams. Methanolysis is particularly advantageous because impurities in the monomer ...

item.title: Exploiting Ruthenium(II) Polypyridyl Complexes to Advance Intracellular Photoredox Catalysis as an Antimicrobial Strategy
item.description: As antibiotic resistant infections become ever more prevalent, the WHO has designated the antimicrobial resistance (AMR) crisis as a silent epidemic. With over 1.1 million deaths directly caused by AMR in 2021 and a further 4.7 million associated deaths ...

item.title: Untargeted metabolomic profiling of glioblastoma invasion using multi-modal mass spectrometry
item.description: 2 ABSTRACT: Isocitrate dehydrogenase wild-type glioblastoma (GBM) is an aggressive brain tumour characterized by complex metabolic mechanisms underlying its invasive behaviour. Single-cell metabolomics was performed to characterize metabolic signatures of ...

item.title: Scanning lanthanoid magnetic susceptibility tensors by spy molecules using paramagnetic fast magicangle spinning NMR spectroscopy
item.description: Magnetic properties of lanthanoid ions at the atomic level are challenging to derive despite the wealth of chemical and structural information encoded in them. We herein report protondetected paramagnetic solid-state NMR experiments at magic-angle ...
